intro.frame2: Agent, we've been tracking Rattlesnake Corp for months. Their reach extends further than anyone imagined — financial systems, infrastructure, government contracts.
intro.frame3: Their CEO, known only as 'Venomous Snake', has been developing something dangerous. A platform that could compromise every secure network in the country. We can't let that happen.
intro.frame4: Your mission: infiltrate their headquarters. Use their own systems against them. Gather the evidence. Get out. Simple in theory — less so in practice.
intro.frame5: Hey there! I'm CIPHER — your AI partner on this op. Think of me as the world's most helpful assistant with significantly better taste in music than your Handler.
intro.frame6: Their entire security infrastructure runs on Python scripts. Learn the language, and you'll own this building. I'll guide you every step of the way — no prior coding experience needed.
intro.frame7: Ready? Every great programmer started exactly where you are now. Let's make history. The lobby is waiting.

f1.cipher: Ground floor. This is base camp — the fundamentals. Eight terminals, eight challenges, all about Python basics.
f1.note: Print statements, variables, data types. Small tools, but these are the building blocks of everything that follows.
f1.hint: Tip: hover any terminal to see which concept it covers. Start anywhere — I'll be right here.

f2.cipher: Nicely done on the lobby. Floor 2: string manipulation. Their HR system stores everything as text — names, codes, reports.
f2.note: Once you can slice and format strings, their personnel files are an open book. F-strings are your new best friend.
f2.hint: Tip: Python's string methods are powerful. `.upper()`, `.split()`, `.replace()` — all fair game up here.

f3.cipher: Floor 3. The building's access-control logic. This is where booleans and conditionals live.
f3.note: Every locked door has a condition: if badge is valid, then open. Master `if/elif/else` and you'll think like their security architects.
f3.hint: Tip: watch out for operator precedence — `and`, `or`, `not`. A misplaced `not` can flip your entire logic chain.

f4.cipher: The patrol scheduling system. Loops are how they automate guard rotations — and loops are how we'll unravel it.
f4.note: `for` loops, `while` loops, `break`, `continue`, `range()`. The building's rhythms are written in repetition.
f4.hint: Tip: an infinite `while True` loop with a well-placed `break` is often the cleanest solution up here.

f5.cipher: Halfway there. The comms center. Functions are how their engineers kept the codebase maintainable — and how we'll reuse our attack vectors.
f5.note: Define once, call everywhere. `def`, `return`, `*args`, `**kwargs` — functions are the architecture of clean code.
f5.hint: Tip: a function that does exactly one thing is easier to test, debug, and reuse. The engineers here weren't quite that disciplined. You can do better.

f6.cipher: The inventory system. Lists, dictionaries, tuples, sets — Python's core data structures power everything stored in this building.
f6.note: Their inventory is a dictionary of dictionaries. Nested, layered, waiting for someone who knows how to navigate it.
f6.hint: Tip: `dict.get(key, default)` is safer than direct access. Missing keys won't crash a cautious operative.

f7.cipher: Floor 7 — the intelligence archive. File I/O and data parsing. Their most sensitive documents are sitting right here.
f7.note: CSV files, JSON configs, log files. Python reads them all. Once you can parse their formats, their intel is your intel.
f7.hint: Tip: always use `with open(...)` to handle files. It closes cleanly even when things go wrong — and in the field, things always go wrong.

f8.cipher: The mission planning floor. Exception handling: how their system reacts when things break. Every robust system anticipates failure.
f8.note: `try`, `except`, `finally`, custom exceptions — this is defensive programming. Their system has vulnerabilities here we can exploit.
f8.hint: Tip: catch specific exceptions, not bare `except`. Swallowing all errors blindly is how security leaks happen.

f9.cipher: Floor 9 — operations. Modules, lambdas, map and filter, regular expressions. This is where experienced developers spend their time.
f9.note: Their automation scripts import a dozen custom modules. Understanding the module system means understanding their full attack surface.
f9.hint: Tip: `re.compile()` your patterns if you use them more than once. Efficiency matters when you're running recon at scale.

f10.cipher: Executive floor. Object-oriented programming. Their core platform is built on class hierarchies their own team barely understands.
f10.note: Classes, inheritance, polymorphism, dunder methods. The architecture of Rattlesnake Corp's entire product is exposed on this floor.
f10.hint: Tip: when overriding a parent method, call `super()` unless you have a very good reason not to. Respect the chain.

f11.cipher: Almost there. The QA lab. Type hints, dataclasses, and unit testing. This is how professional engineers prove their code works.
f11.note: Their test suite has significant gaps — gaps we've been able to exploit. Writing tests isn't just good practice; it's how you understand a system.
f11.hint: Tip: a failing test is not a failure — it's information. The QA team here forgot that. Don't make their mistake.

f12.cipher: This is it. The penthouse. Venomous Snake is right there. And this floor covers the most advanced Python — generators, decorators, async/await, structural pattern matching.
f12.note: The final layer of their security runs on concepts most developers never fully master. You've come this far. You're ready.
f12.hint: Whatever happens next — I've got your back. Show them what you've learned.

boss.frame1: So. They actually sent someone. A programmer, no less. How... quaint.
boss.frame2: You've picked a few locks, solved a few puzzles. Don't mistake that for skill. This system was built by the best engineers money can buy. You are out of your depth.
boss.frame3: Don't listen to him. He's scared. You've cracked eleven floors of his most guarded systems. He knows exactly how much trouble he's in.
boss.frame4: I admire the persistence — truly. But this final layer? Generators, decorators, async concurrency. This isn't a classroom exercise. This is my masterwork. You can't understand it.
boss.frame5: Oh, he's monologuing. Classic. That's what people do when they've already lost. You've got everything you need. Show them what you've learned.
boss.frame6: This is what all those challenges were for. Every concept, every error message, every hint you pushed through — it all leads here. Now go write some code.

victory.frame1: [ SYSTEM BREACH — RATTLESNAKE CORP MAINFRAME — ACCESS GRANTED ]
victory.frame2: This... this isn't possible. Who — how did you even — this system was impenetrable. I built it myself.
victory.frame3: You did it! Every single algorithm, every data structure, every pattern they threw at you — and you solved them all. I am genuinely impressed. And I'm a very hard AI to impress.
victory.frame4: We're transmitting the evidence now. Financial records, surveillance data, the whole scheme. Rattlesnake Corp is finished. You finished them — with code.
victory.frame5: Mission accomplished, Agent. Outstanding work. The building is secure, the data is transmitted, and Venomous Snake is in custody. You've earned this.

gameover.frame2: Hey — setbacks happen. Every great programmer has a stack of failed attempts behind them. The question is whether you get back up.
gameover.frame3: I'll be right here when you're ready to try again. The building isn't going anywhere. Neither am I.

step.movement: Use WASD or the arrow keys to move around. Try walking towards that terminal — the glowing one! I'll wait.
step.interaction: Nice moves! Now press E or Space to interact with objects. Give that terminal a go — it won't bite. Probably.
step.terminalBasics: Welcome to your hacking terminal! This is where the magic happens. You write Python code right here in the editor. Try typing: print('hello') — then hit Run.
step.codeSubmit: See those buttons? 'Run' tests your code without submitting. 'Submit' locks in your answer. Try Run first, check the output, then Submit when you're confident.
step.firstSuccess: You just wrote your first working hack! Seriously — that's not nothing. See that XP bar? Every challenge you complete fills it up. Keep going and you'll level up fast.

tutorial.skipConfirm: Skip the whole tutorial? You can restart it from Settings any time.

credits.title: Mission Complete
credits.subtitle: Venomous Snake's empire has fallen.
credits.newGamePlus: New Game+ — Keep your skills, start fresh
